reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P by submitting a beacon block with execution requests that cause ProcessDepositRequests to fail (e.g., state operation errors during pending deposit append). The error is returned as raw execReqErr (L517) instead of invalidBlock, bypassing proper fork choice invalidation and potentially allowing the invalid block to propagate. This affects consensus safety as improperly validated blocks may not be rejected by fork choice. Severity: High based on potential for network confusion and improper block handling affecting >33% of Prysm nodes (31% network share).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Electra spec requires all deposit validation failures to result in block invalidation. Execution requests processing is part of Electra/Fusaka upgrade (EIP-6110 deposit processing).
